Use this document to ask questions, coordinate and update.

Trello: [https://trello.com/invite/b/69af4bd6a53e2fe78265a076/ATTIc3283748f8c26ea081ac8a0cbcf1dd9eB16B5A8C/project](https://trello.com/invite/b/69af4bd6a53e2fe78265a076/ATTIc3283748f8c26ea081ac8a0cbcf1dd9eB16B5A8C/project)

RS \- **28/04** \- Local VBox adaptors created. Production Ubuntu VM provisioned and OS installed. Production Win25 Server VM provisioned and OS installed. 

JF \- Palo Alto: The firewall is accessible from 192.168.1.1 and the PC needs to be within this subnet. PC is 192.168.1.2 for now and is patched in via port PA-11.   
username: admin   
password: Password1  
Static IPs were set on the three needed interfaces. Three different network zones were configured for each interface. MGT interface is the physical interface on the firewall.   
A Virtual Router with a static route was established, as well as two service routes. DNS and Palo Alto Networks Services. 

RS \- **29/04** \- Ubuntu & Windows Server VM’s further configured to maximum possible point. Further configuration not possible until Firewall and switch have been configured. Will resume as soon as possible.

Also updated MSProject file and changelog. (ver 0.1) Check RS Worknotes for further info.

TODO: 

- Resources to assign themselves to their tasks in MSProject and keep progress updated. (0%, 25%, 50%, 100% on individual tasks) If necessary, I can do this if progress is communicated in this doc.  
- Trello (Linked above) will need to be maintained by one resource and cards added for various tasks determined by the MSProject, as well as more granular milestones from readme for tasks. Should be straightforward. Once set up, resources can mark tasks as complete as they are completed.  
- Repo will need to be created on Github by one resource and maintained. This will include configurations, documentation and notes.  
- All-hands in-person meeting to complete phase one (project planning, roles, meeting minutes and to establish baselines for team performance, success measures and milestones/goals)  
    
  Ongoing: Weekly meetings to ensure the project (tech AND management) is progressing smoothly and any adjustments or reassignments can be made.

**Recommended:** Consider taking regular snapshots/backups (with versioning/date/times) whenever configuring hardware or VM’s so that they can be restored if something goes wrong. **Switch config should always be exported after every session as other students may wipe switch config.**

RS \- **01/05** \- Tidied up the project folder and updated project scope. Awaiting Firewall and Switch config before continuing config of Ubuntu/Elastic & Windows Server.

JF \- **01/05** **Palo Alto Firewall**  
Configured Source NAT from LAN to WAN using PAT. NAT Policy created.   
Configured Security Policy allowing traffic through the Firewall.   
Configured Security Policy for DMZ (Linux Web Server)  
Tested from CLI. Used PuTTY to connect via ssh. Login: admin, Password1.   
Tested 192.168.100.10 to 8.8.8.8 security policy. Got Allow-Internal-Outbound, what we were looking for. Also tested the above ips for NAT policy. Got LAN-to-WAN-NAT which proves the PAT (Source NAT) is working. 

RS \- **05/05** \- Updated MSProject with assigned resources for all phase two tasks

RS \- **08/05** \- No working firewall \- will need to reconfigure SIEM VBox interface and begin work using NIC interface to make progress, taking care to document changes that will need to be made to move over to firewall network at a later date.

JF \- **08/05** \- Firewall’s basic configuration was finalised. Justin helped us with Internet connectivity. 

JF \- **12/05:** **Final Basic Config:**

| Ethernet1/1 | Ethernet1/2 | Ethernet1/3 |
| :---- | :---- | :---- |
| **Virtual Router:** V-Router **Security Zone:** Outside **IPv4:** 192.168.108.101/24 | **Virtual Router:** V-Router **Security Zone:** Inside **IPv4:** 192.168.100.1/24 **Management Profile:** inside-management-profile | **Virtual Router:** V-Router **Security Zone:** DMZ **IPv4:** 192.168.50.1/24 |

**V-Router:** For all three interfaces.  
	**Static Route:**

| Name | Destination | Interface | Type | Value |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Default-route | 0.0.0.0/0 | ethernet1/1 | ip-address | 192.168.108.1 |

**Security Policy:**  
**General:**  
	**Name:**	inside\_to\_outside			**Rule Type:** universal (default)  
**Source:**  
	**Source Zone:**	Any  
**Destination:**   
	**Destination Zone:** Outside  
**Service/URL Category:**  
	**Service:** application-default

**NAT Policy:**  
**General:**  
	**Name:** inside\_to\_outside			**NAT Type:** ipv4  
**Original Packet:**  
	**Source Zone:** inside				**Destination Zone:** Outside  
							**Destination Interface:** ethernet1/1  
**Translated Packet:**  
	**Translation Type:** Dynamic IP And Port	**Address Type:** Interface Address  
	**Interface:** ethernet1/1			**IP Address:** None

JF \- 12/05 \- A DNS Proxy was set up and committed.   
**Name:** Internal-DNS-Proxy		**Interface:** ethernet1/1 \*\*\*unsure\*\*\*  
**Inheritance Source:** None  
**Primary:** 8.8.8.8			**Secondary:** 192.168.210.2  
**Static Entries tab:**  
**Add:**   
**Name:**	SIEM-Record			**FQDN:** [siem.rs](http://siem.rs).local  
**Add:** 192.168.100.200  
**Changes to Firewall were Committed\!**

Windows machine’s DNS was reconfigured to go through the Firewall. IP address was left as 192.168.100.10, but DNS was changed to 192.168.100.1, the default gateway of the Firewall.   
Once DNS was configured the following was successful:  
![][image1]  
**We still need to ensure Windows clients can connect to the DNS.** 

**We are more likely to use VLANs in the real world. For this project we will be vlanning instead of using another Firewall interface.** 

CR/JF **13/05** \- Switch has been configured with two VLAN Zones (VLAN 2/Internal, VLAN 3/DMZ). The Internal VLAN Zone will be where all our servers go through. Switch Trunk port has been configure to the Firewall through switch port 24\. Firewall needs to be configured for the VLANs still

RS \- **12/05** \- Elastic server setup with kibana, SSLs, and defender integration. Agent shows as healthy. Snapshot taken. Next step is to set up Windows Server as Domain Controller (6.6 in Prototype documentation. Cross check with Production docs)  
**Device \> Setup \> Interfaces:** Click *Management* under interface name. Change 

**When updating documents in Google Drive, please remember to create a new versioned document and update the changelog to reflect the changes or additions you made.**

RS \- **13-05-26**

Elastic running. All steps of phase two complete.

TODO:   
Win11 Client and Server ‘25 to be run in bridged adaptor mode if not already. All configuration completed where possible and Elastic Defend deployed on Win 11 and Server ‘25. (Step 6.6.3 of Prototype, 5.3 of Production, remember to use the stated “--insecure” flag or this will not work due to the SSL’s being self-signed) WinServer also needs to be promoted to domain controller with DNS if it hasn’t already. 

Firewall needs to be configured for the VLANs.

RS will begin work on Web server

PA Firewall and switch logs need to be configured to be sent to SIEM/Elastic. PA is step 7 of Production doc. Switch logs process are in step 9.11 of Production doc.

**\[Be extremely careful during this phase as it is where things will likely go wrong and can take a long time to troubleshoot. Take snapshots of VMs before starting work, and after key features have been confirmed as operational\]**

Web server work complete. Ready to connect to network once Switch/VLAN/Firewall DMZ config is complete.

Name webdmz

U: rik

P: Password1

Webserver and SIEM VMs have been backed up to SSD \#3

15-05 \- RS

- Completer monthly cybersecurity report  
- Working on Phishing assessment

**15-05:**   
JF \- IP address for ethernet1/2 (Trunk port on Firewall) was removed and a subinterface was created.   
Subinterface details:

* Name: ethernet1/2.2  
* Tag: 2  
* V-Router  
* Inside  
* IPv4: 192.168.100.1/24

Commit new settings\! Ran into an error. DHCP was still trying to go through ethernet1/2 which no longer has an IP assigned. The old DHCP Server was deleted and a new one created using our new VLAN 2\.    
DHCP Server: ethernet1/2.2 \- 192.168.100.50-192.168.100.60 range  
Options Tab: Gateway \- 192.168.100.1, Subnet: 255.255.255.0, Primary DNS: 8.8.8.8, Secondary: 192.168.100.100, NTP: 192.168.100.100  
Commit was successful. Was able to connect to the internet but not access the management site of the Firewall.   
Re-connected via the management port and attached the Interface Management profile to the new interface (ethernet1/2.2).   
VLAN 3 will still need to be created for the DMZ server.

Testing Server and Client connectivity:  
**Windows Server has the internet and is able to reach the Firewall Management via a browser.** 

**15/05**  
CR \- Windows Server up and running and configured to connect to Switch/Firewall network.  
Windows Server IP addressing \-  
IP \- 192.168.100.100/24  
Gateway \- 192.168.100.1  
DNS \- 127.0.0.1, 8.8.8.8

Connectivity to Firewall \-   
![][image2]  
Server has been promoted to Domain Controller \-  
Hostname \- crserver  
Domain \- cr.local

DNS initial configuration has been done but will need further configuration

Windows Client installed and configured  
IP \- 192.168.100.20/24  
Gateway \- 192.168.100.1  
DNS \- 192.168.100.100, 8.8.8.8  
Connection to Firewall \-

![][image3]

Client has been connected to Domain Controller.

**Need to prove all network devices can communicate to complete phase02.** 

**19/05:**  
The VDI file for Ubuntu SIEM Server was used to open the server from a different PC as Rik was absent today. A snapshot was taken before making any changes (Rik\_settings). 

I noticed the SIEM has an IP of 192.168.208.48, but this is not used within our network. As discussed earlier, we planned to use the Production topology until further confirming IP addresses, however, [192.168.208.XXX](http://192.168.208.XXX) is not used for connecting to the E208 Switch, (for Internet connection)  [192.168.108.XXX](http://192.168.108.XXX) is. 

CR and JF changed the netplan documentation to change the IP address to 192.168.100.200, as per the topology diagram. 

![][image4]

![][image5]

![][image6]  
CR: Set up Windows Server as DNS Server on 192.168.100.100. DNS forwarder set in Server Manager as 8.8.8.8

RS \- 20 \- 05 \- 26

- Verified DMZ/Web server config  
- Verified elastic/kibana still functional and now presenting enrolment token for powershell command

#### 6.6.3 Install Elastic Defend Agent on Windows 11 Client: **(Only after network has been tested/confirmed and Win Server DNS is confirmed as functional.)**

Start-BitsTransfer \-Source https://artifacts.elastic.co/downloads/beats/elastic-agent/elastic-agent-9.4.1-windows-x86\_64.zip \-OutFile elastic-agent-9.4.1-windows-x86\_64.zip   
Expand-Archive .\\elastic-agent-9.4.1-windows-x86\_64.zip \-DestinationPath .  
cd elastic-agent-9.4.1-windows-x86\_64  
.\\elastic-agent.exe install \--url=https://192.168.100.200:8220 \--enrollment-token=eko5MkdwNEJBMU56TnRCbC1XbDU6eXNLcG9fdnd3UlpKSlIyZVRCSmQzZw== \--insecure

**JF \- PA-FW 20-05:**

Following Production instructions:

Time was already set for Melb/Aus. Dynamic updates were checked and the latest version of Applications and threats was installed, as it was already downloaded.   
Security policy did not need to be changed \- matches github configuration. NAT policy name was changed to NAT\_inside\_to\_outside and on the Translated Packet tab our IP address that takes us out to the internet (192.168.108.101/24) was added.   
Commit changes. 

7.1 Syslog config  
**Device \> setup \> Services \> service route config**   
Source Interface was set to our sub interface E1/2.2, 192.168.100.1/24  
I need to set up more sub-interfaces for DMZ in the future \*\*\*

**Device \> Server Profiles \> Syslog**  
Add \- Name: SyslogToUbuntuSIEM  
Add \- Name: UbuntuServerSIEM  
	Syslog Server: 192.168.100.200  
	Transport: TCP  
	Port: 9001  
	Format: IETF  
	Facility: LOG\_USER

Log forwarding Profile, **Objects \> Log Forwarding**  
Add \- UbuntuSIEMProfile and add the following as traffic and filter for all logs:  
LogTraffic and LogThreat  
Attach Server Profile to each log above (Traffic and Threat). 

inside\_to\_outside security policy was updated. On the **Actions** tab Log Forwarding is via *UbuntuSIEMProfile*. 

To pick up Friday, 7.2 Elastic Integration. 

RS \- 22-05-26

- Checked Switch & PA config. Still some issues. Will go through config of both and resolve problems if possible  
- Fixed/tidied up switch & PA config. VLANs look good. Subinterfaces should be fixed and zones (particularly associated with DMZ) on PA have been fixed. Looks ready to test. Cabling: LAN Hosts \- Interfaces 1-8. DMZ \- 9 \- 12\. Trunk \- To PA: Interface 24\. Webserver/DMZ will need to be run on its own host and patched into any interface 9 \- 12\.
